title: Capability Contract
description: Machine-readable contract for capability monitoring. A capability is healthy ONLY if its intended output is produced, correct, and on time (Founder directive: replace heartbeat monitoring with capability monitoring). Freshness/coverage thresholds that depend on VAJRA's real cadence are declared but left null (UNKNOWN) until calibrated from observed reality — they are never guessed.

artifact.description: The concrete output that proves the capability works, e.g. 'fresh quote observations in raw store'.

healthy_definition.description: Plain statement of health in terms of produced+correct+on-time output.

correctness_checks.description: Named checks (reference SPEC-05 rule ids) that must pass for output to count as correct.

freshness.description: On-time criterion. Thresholds null until calibrated from observed cadence.

max_staleness_seconds.description: null = UNKNOWN, to be set from measured cadence + tolerance.

expected_cadence.description: e.g. 'per-tick during continuous session'; null = UNKNOWN.

session_aware.description: If true, freshness is only evaluated during active session windows.

coverage.description: Optional completeness criterion (e.g. instruments/venues that must be covered). null members = UNKNOWN.

failure_signals.description: Observable conditions that mark the capability UNHEALTHY (e.g. 'no output within max_staleness_seconds during session').

status.description: Current verified status. 'absent' = component does not exist in the observable environment.

status_evidence.description: Artifact/citation for the current status. Required to claim 'healthy' (Constitution Law 2/3).
